# UC-14.4.16 · IoT Device Certificate Expiry

## Description

Expired device certs break TLS to cloud and brick OTA; proactive rotation avoids mass outages.

## Value

Expired device certs break TLS to cloud and brick OTA; proactive rotation avoids mass outages.

## Implementation

Ingest cert metadata from AWS IoT / Azure DPS / custom PKI. Alert at 45, 14, 7 days. Automate renewal jobs.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: PKI portal, device shadow attributes.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="iot:cert_inventory"` (device_id, not_after).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest cert metadata from AWS IoT / Azure DPS / custom PKI. Alert at 45, 14, 7 days. Automate renewal jobs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iot sourcetype="iot:cert_inventory"
| eval days_left=round((strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 45
| table device_id, not_after, days_left
| sort days_left
```

Understanding this SPL

**IoT Device Certificate Expiry** — Expired device certs break TLS to cloud and brick OTA; proactive rotation avoids mass outages.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="iot:cert_inventory"` (device_id, not_after). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): PKI portal, device shadow attributes. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iot; **sourcetype**: iot:cert_inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iot, sourcetype="iot:cert_inventory". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_left** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_left < 45` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **IoT Device Certificate Expiry**): table device_id, not_after, days_left
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare device counts, last-seen times, and health fields to the IoT platform console (for example AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub, or Google Cloud IoT) for the same period. Verify API polling intervals and that offline rules match your operational definition.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (certs expiring), Timeline (renewal window), Single value (devices <30d).

## SPL

```spl
index=iot sourcetype="iot:cert_inventory"
| eval days_left=round((strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 45
| table device_id, not_after, days_left
| sort days_left
```

Table (certs expiring), Timeline (renewal window), Single value (devices <30d).
